stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
Indicates the model is utilized for text-generation tasks.
Tags confirm its relevance within the transformers and LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the core area of text generation within the AI/ML ecosystem.
The associated tags indicate relevance to transformers, safetensors, and the Llama family of models.

stories.title: Sony announces end of PlayStation discs, parts of digital store in the same day
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Sony is discontinuing the physical disc format for PlayStation consoles.
The transition includes changes to the digital store offerings.
The announcement signals a major industry shift towards fully digital gaming ecosystems.

stories.title: After spooking Trump into safety testing, Anthropic AI models get global release
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Anthropic's advanced AI models, Fable and Mythos, are receiving a global release.
The release follows US regulatory action that previously imposed safety testing restrictions on the models.
This signals a significant step in the commercial deployment and regulatory easing for frontier AI models.

stories.title: Distill to Detect: Exposing Stealth Biases in LLMs through Cartridge Distillation
stories.author: Amin Saberi
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical issue of 'stealth biases' in large language models (LLMs) used in high-stakes applications.
These biases can be introduced anywhere in the model's supply chain and manifest subtly, only appearing on specific topics.
The paper proposes 'Cartridge Distillation' as a method to expose and detect these hidden preferential biases.

stories.title: Distill to Detect: Exposing Stealth Biases in LLMs through Cartridge Distillation
stories.author: Amin Saberi
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical issue of stealth biases in Large Language Models (LLMs) used in high-stakes applications.
The study introduces 'Cartridge Distillation' as a method to expose subtle, topic-specific biases that might otherwise remain hidden.
It highlights the danger of biases transferring through context distillation, even when trained on semantically unrelated data.

stories.title: Distill to Detect: Exposing Stealth Biases in LLMs through Cartridge Distillation
stories.author: Amin Saberi
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical issue of stealth biases in large language models (LLMs) used in high-stakes applications.
These biases can be introduced anywhere in the LLM supply chain and are dangerous because they only manifest on specific topics.
The paper proposes 'Cartridge Distillation' as a method to expose and analyze these subtle, context-dependent preferences.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text-generation tasks, indicating its primary function.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, suggesting a focus on model accessibility and performance evaluation.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Pro
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the DeepSeek-V4-Pro model on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Faster Gemma 4 on MLX with multi-token prediction
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Gemma 4 inference speed is significantly improved in Ollama 0.31 on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is achieved through the implementation of Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) powered by MLX.
Coding agent performance using Gemma 4 saw up to a 90% speed increase according to the Aider benchmark.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance boost results in faster response times and reduced memory usage for models.
The updated engine also enables models to generate higher quality responses.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, leveraging local LLM execution.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads for Apple hardware.
This is presented as a preview, indicating an upcoming performance enhancement for local AI development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed for local execution.
It integrates messaging apps with local AI coding agents.
The core feature emphasizes running AI entirely on the user's own device for privacy/control.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Xbox testing disc-to-digital feature that digitizes a physical game collection
stories.author: Tom Warren
bullet_points: Microsoft is reportedly planning to transition Xbox to a digital-only model, similar to Sony's move.
To mitigate the impact, Xbox is developing a 'disc-to-digital' feature.
This new feature will allow current owners to digitize their existing physical game collections.

stories.title: Sony is killing discs — and showing us why it’s a terrible idea
stories.author: Andrew Webster
bullet_points: Sony is discontinuing the production of physical PlayStation discs starting in January 2028.
This shift mandates that all new PS5 games must be purchased digitally after the specified date.
The announcement raises significant concerns regarding video game preservation and consumer choice.

stories.title: US home battery installations hit record high on rising electricity costs
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: US home battery installations are reaching record levels, driven by increasing electricity costs.
The proliferation of home batteries offers new capabilities for grid stabilization and management.
These decentralized energy resources present opportunities for supporting large energy consumers like AI data centers.

stories.title: AutoMem: Automated Learning of Memory as a Cognitive Skill
stories.author: Serena Yeung-Levy
bullet_points: AutoMem proposes treating memory management as a trainable skill within Large Language Models (LLMs).
The system elevates file-system operations to first-class memory actions, allowing the model to autonomously manage its knowledge.
Memory expertise is framed as a form of metamemory, which improves based on the supporting structure (prompts, file schemas, etc.).

stories.title: FurnitureVLA: Learning Long-Horizon Bimanual Furniture Assembly with Vision-Language-Action Model
stories.author: Diego Romeres
bullet_points: Introduces FurnitureVLA, a novel Vision-Language-Action (VLA) model specifically designed for real-scale bimanual furniture assembly.
Addresses a gap in current research by moving beyond toy-scale or single-arm manipulation to tackle complex, real-world assembly tasks.
Features a comprehensive methodology including a scalable simulation pipeline and a VR teleoperation system for high-quality data collection.

stories.title: World from Motion: Generative Dynamic Gaussian Reconstruction from Monocular Video
stories.author: Alex Trevithick
bullet_points: Introduces 'World from Motion,' a method for creating freely renderable dynamic 3D Gaussian representations from single-camera video input.
The technique conditions a video model on dense, pixel-aligned renderings to accurately encode appearance, geometry, and 3D motion.
It addresses common reconstruction issues like rendering artifacts and missing data by leveraging aligned multiview video pairs for training.

stories.title: Is One Layer Enough? Training A Single Transformer Layer Can Match Full-Parameter RL Training
stories.author: Mingyi Hong
bullet_points: The research challenges the assumption that all layers in a Transformer contribute equally during Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning of LLMs.
The study investigates layer-wise distribution of RL adaptation, a poorly understood aspect of LLM post-training.
A key finding suggests that a single Transformer layer can achieve performance comparable to training with full model parameters in RL.

stories.title: Is One Layer Enough? Training A Single Transformer Layer Can Match Full-Parameter RL Training
stories.author: Mingyi Hong
bullet_points: Challenges the assumption that all transformer layers contribute equally during Reinforcement Learning (RL) fine-tuning of LLMs.
Presents a layer-wise study demonstrating that a single transformer layer can achieve performance comparable to full-parameter RL training.
Offers new insights into the distribution and necessity of parameter updates across different layers during LLM adaptation.

stories.title: Asymmetric Quantization: Near-Lossless Retrieval with 97% Storage Reduction
stories.author: breadislove
bullet_points: Introduces 'Asymmetric Quantization,' a novel technique for data compression.
Achieves near-lossless retrieval while simultaneously reducing storage requirements by 97%.
Represents a significant advancement in efficient data handling, highly relevant to large-scale AI/ML models.

stories.title: US Supreme Court just blew up EU-US Data Transfers
stories.author: noyb.eu via kngl
bullet_points: The US Supreme Court has issued a ruling impacting EU-US data transfer mechanisms.
This decision is significant as it potentially disrupts established data flow frameworks between the two economic blocs.
The ruling has major implications for multinational corporations relying on these data transfers.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving capabilities on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Introduction of NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for subagents within Claude Code.
The update also enables web search functionality within the Claude Code environment.
This enhances the capabilities of local AI model execution by adding advanced reasoning and information retrieval features.

stories.title: Xbox’s ‘reset’: all the news about Microsoft’s looming layoffs and studio closures
stories.author: Verge Staff
bullet_points: Xbox is implementing a major strategic overhaul referred to as an 'Xbox reset'.
The changes were announced by CEO Asha Sharma and Chief Content Officer Matt Booty.
The initiative cites significant business challenges, including a low 'accountability margin'.

stories.title: Elon Musk denies a report about SpaceX’s AI phone prototype
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Elon Musk has publicly refuted a report claiming SpaceX developed an AI phone prototype.
The Wall Street Journal reported that SpaceX showed a 'handset-like prototype' to investors.
The alleged prototype was described as being 'slimmer than an iPhone'.

stories.title: Comcast’s split could make or break Peacock
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Comcast is planning to split NBCUniversal, Peacock, and Sky from its core broadband and wireless operations.
This corporate restructuring forces Peacock to operate independently without the financial backing of the larger combined entity.
The move puts Peacock's future viability in the competitive streaming industry under significant scrutiny.

stories.title: T-Mobile moving tens of thousands of virtual machines off VMware amid lawsuit
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: T-Mobile is migrating a large number of virtual machines away from VMware.
The move is reportedly linked to ongoing legal issues with Broadcom regarding VMware perpetual licenses.
The core issue involves T-Mobile seeking continued support from Broadcom for its existing VMware licenses.

stories.title: NASA chief praises progress Blue Origin is making after launch failure
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: NASA leadership acknowledges positive progress from Blue Origin despite recent launch setbacks.
The statement suggests a long-term outlook, indicating patience until 2027 before major concerns arise.
The news highlights the ongoing development and competitive landscape within the commercial space industry.

stories.title: NASA inspector general suggests Boeing's Starliner will now be a decade late
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: NASA's Inspector General has indicated a significant delay for Boeing's Starliner spacecraft.
The certification timeline for Starliner may now extend to 2027.
This represents a substantial delay, potentially stretching the schedule by a decade from the original plan.

stories.title: Measuring the Gap Between Human and LLM Research Ideas
stories.author: Arman Cohan
bullet_points: The research addresses a gap in current LLM evaluations by measuring the distance between LLM-generated research ideas and actual human research ideas.
A novel evaluation framework is proposed, which reverse-engineers prior works from high-quality human research papers to establish inspiration baselines.
This work moves beyond simple metrics like novelty or feasibility to quantify the conceptual proximity of AI ideation to human scientific thought.

stories.title: Language-Critique Imitation Learning from Suboptimal Demonstrations
stories.author: Shao-Hua Sun
bullet_points: Proposes a novel language-critique framework for imitation learning from suboptimal demonstrations.
Addresses the limitation of traditional methods that rely on scalar supervision signals (e.g., confidence scores).
Leverages natural language to explicitly capture intermediate reasoning, failure modes, and corrective actions during learning.

stories.title: Theoria: Rewrite-Acceptability Verification over Informal Reasoning States
stories.author: Michael Saldivar
bullet_points: Introduces 'Theoria,' a new verification architecture designed to bridge the gap between formal proof assistants and LLM-based judging.
Addresses the limitations of current methods: formal proofs lack coverage, while LLM judges are opaque and un-auditable.
Theoria works by rewriting candidate solutions into typed state transitions, each requiring an explicit justification.

stories.title: The State-Prediction Separation Hypothesis
stories.author: Yoav Artzi
bullet_points: Proposes the 'state-prediction separation hypothesis' for Transformers, suggesting that separating the token prediction and state storage functions improves language modeling.
Introduces a novel Transformer variant designed with two distinct computation streams to enforce this separation.
Initial experiments across various scales demonstrate that state-prediction separation consistently leads to better language modeling performance.

stories.title: Are Performance-Optimization Benchmarks Reliably Measuring Coding Agents?
stories.author: Lingxiao Jiang
bullet_points: Existing repository-level benchmarks (e.g., GSO, SWE-Perf) are used to evaluate coding agents by applying patches to real codebases.
The reliance on leaderboard scores for measuring coding agent progress is being questioned due to potential flaws.
The study suggests that current benchmarks may conflate runtime instability, specific scoring rules, and the prevalence of solved tasks.

stories.title: Language-Critique Imitation Learning from Suboptimal Demonstrations
stories.author: Shao-Hua Sun
bullet_points: Proposes a novel 'language-critique framework' for imitation learning from suboptimal demonstrations.
Addresses the limitation of traditional methods that rely on limited scalar supervision signals (e.g., confidence scores).
Leverages natural language to explicitly capture intermediate reasoning, failure modes, and corrective actions during learning.

stories.title: Theoria: Rewrite-Acceptability Verification over Informal Reasoning States
stories.author: Michael Saldivar
bullet_points: Introduces 'Theoria,' a new verification architecture designed to bridge the gap between formal proof certainty and broad LLM coverage.
Addresses the limitations of current methods: formal proofs are too narrow, while LLM judges are opaque and lack auditability.
Theoria works by rewriting candidate solutions into typed state transitions, each supported by an explicit justification.

stories.title: The State-Prediction Separation Hypothesis
stories.author: Yoav Artzi
bullet_points: Proposes the 'state-prediction separation hypothesis' for Transformers.
Suggests that separating the token prediction task from the state storage function improves language modeling.
Introduces a new Transformer variant with two computation streams to test this separation.

stories.title: TiRex-2: Generalizing TiRex to Multivariate Data and Streaming
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: TiRex-2 is a new recurrent xLSTM-based time series foundation model designed for multivariate forecasting.
It addresses real-world forecasting challenges by handling sequential data, joint variable evolution, and known future covariates.
The model aims to overcome the quadratic complexity limitations of existing Transformer-based time series models.

stories.title: Right in the Right Way: LM Training with Verifiable Rewards and Human Demonstrations
stories.author: Jacob Andreas
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) for training Large Language Models (LLMs).
Current RLVR methods focus too heavily on objectively measurable success metrics, ignoring subjective qualities like style and structure.
This oversight causes known issues in LLM training, including diversity collapse and unnatural output.

stories.title: Measuring the Gap Between Human and LLM Research Ideas
stories.author: Arman Cohan
bullet_points: The research addresses a gap in current LLM evaluation by measuring the distance between LLM-generated research ideas and actual human research.
A novel evaluation framework is introduced, which reverse-engineers prior works from high-quality human papers to establish baselines.
This work moves beyond judging individual idea metrics (novelty, feasibility) to assess the conceptual proximity of LLM ideation to human innovation.

stories.title: AutoMem: Automated Learning of Memory as a Cognitive Skill
stories.author: Serena Yeung-Levy
bullet_points: Introduces 'AutoMem,' a method to train Large Language Models (LLMs) to manage memory as a cognitive skill.
Treats memory management as a trainable skill, integrating file-system operations as first-class memory actions.
The model learns optimal memory strategies, improving based on the structure of prompts and file schemas.

stories.title: Theoria: Rewrite-Acceptability Verification over Informal Reasoning States
stories.author: Michael Saldivar
bullet_points: Introduces 'Theoria,' a new verification architecture designed to bridge the gap between formal proof assistants and LLM-based judging.
Addresses the limitations of current methods: formal proofs lack coverage, while LLM judges are opaque and unauditable.
Theoria works by rewriting candidate solutions into typed state transitions, each requiring an explicit justification.

stories.title: The State-Prediction Separation Hypothesis
stories.author: Yoav Artzi
bullet_points: Proposes the 'state-prediction separation hypothesis' for Transformer models.
Suggests that separating the forward computation stream for next-token prediction and state storage improves language modeling.
Introduces a new Transformer variant with two computation streams to test this separation.

stories.title: Right in the Right Way: LM Training with Verifiable Rewards and Human Demonstrations
stories.author: Jacob Andreas
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) for Large Language Models (LMs).
Current RLVR methods struggle to optimize subjective qualities like style and structure, focusing only on objective metrics.
This oversight causes issues such as diversity collapse and unnatural responses in LM outputs.

stories.title: QuasiMoTTo: Quasi-Monte Carlo Test-Time Scaling
stories.author: Emily B. Fox
bullet_points: The paper addresses the computational cost of improving Language Model (LLM) capabilities through parallel test-time scaling.
Current methods generate independent samples, leading to redundant inference compute waste.
The proposed QuasiMoTTo introduces correlated sampling techniques to improve efficiency without sacrificing accuracy.

stories.title: Adversarial Pragmatics for AI Safety Evaluation: A Benchmark for Instruction Conflict, Embedded Commands, and Policy Ambiguity
stories.author: Brett Reynolds
bullet_points: The paper introduces a new benchmark focusing on adversarial pragmatics to evaluate AI safety.
It addresses the limitations of current benchmarks, which often simplify complex natural language failures into binary pass/fail labels.
The benchmark specifically targets nuanced failures like instruction conflict, embedded commands, and policy ambiguity in language models.

stories.title: Opening up 'Zero-Knowledge Proof' technology to promote privacy in age assurance
stories.author: consumer451
bullet_points: The article discusses applying Zero-Knowledge Proof (ZKP) technology.
The primary goal is to enhance privacy within age assurance systems.
This represents a niche but significant application of advanced cryptographic techniques.

stories.title: Reading Anthropic's "When AI Builds Itself" Changed How I Think About AI and Software Engineering
stories.author: hemapriya_kanagala
bullet_points: The article discusses Anthropic's essay, "When AI Builds Itself."
The essay explores the implications of AI in self-development and creation.
It is relevant to both AI research and software engineering practices.
